verdict.label: YELLOW · Half size, A-grade only
verdict.desc: The tape is split: a memory-chip rout is dragging the Nasdaq lower while a huge UnitedHealth beat lifts the Dow. TSMC beat but its raised capex spooked the AI trade, SK Hynix fell 11% in Seoul, and the U.S.–Iran conflict is still live — oil sits near one-month highs and the interim peace deal has effectively unraveled. But two cooler inflation prints (soft CPI, then a rare PPI drop) took the edge off. Composite eased to 52 (WARM) and P(≥1% gap) is 34% — just under the 35% trigger. With a live geopolitical tail plus a jumpy chip sector, GREEN is off the table: half size, A-grade only, keep DTE clear of the conflict window and each name’s print.

focus.jbig: Chip rout vs. UNH beat · Iran still live · half size
focus.jtag: Memory-chip selloff drags Nasdaq while UnitedHealth's blowout lifts the Dow — inflation cooled but oil stays high
focus.jnote: Two crosscurrents define the morning. First, a global memory-chip selloff: SK Hynix cratered 11% in Seoul, the Kospi fell more than 6%, and Taiwan Semiconductor slid ~4% even after a record quarter because its raised capex ($60–64B) overshadowed the beat — SMH is off ~2.2% with Arm down ~4%. Second, a UnitedHealth blowout: adjusted EPS of $6.38 vs ~$4.85 expected, revenue of $112B, and full-year guidance raised to $19.50–$20.00, sending the stock up ~7% premarket and lifting Dow futures. Underneath, the Iran conflict is still live — the U.S. ran fresh strikes Wednesday, oil is near one-month highs (~$80 WTI), and the interim peace deal has unraveled. Softer CPI and a rare PPI drop are the offsetting positive. This is a two-sided, headline-heavy tape — deploy carefully, not aggressively.
focus.notdown: UnitedHealth (UNH) is the cleanest post-earnings bid — a genuine double-beat with raised guidance, up ~7% premarket. Healthcare/managed care and defensive Dow names are catching the rotation out of chips. These are the most tradable longs, but the gap must hold through the 9:35 validation and any UNH spread should sit well below the deal-day range. Half size into a still-live Iran backdrop.
focus.notup: Semiconductors (SMH, ARM, MU, WDC, chip complex) are the day’s clear pressure point on the SK Hynix/TSMC capex scare. But selling bear calls into a violent, gapping chip selloff carries brutal snap-back risk on any stabilization headline — and the group has whipsawed all week. The disciplined answer is to wait, not chase the short.
focus.why: Why YELLOW? Composite eased to 52 (WARM) and P(≥1% gap) at 34% sits just under the 35% trigger — both would allow GREEN on their own. What locks GREEN out is the combination: a live, open-ended U.S.–Iran military conflict with oil near one-month highs plus a violent, still-unfolding memory-chip rout. Neither is a scheduled, sizeable-around event. The play: half size, A-grade only, keep DTE clear of the conflict window and each name’s earnings print, and validate at 9:35 before deploying.

how_built.name: Volatility — VIX ~15.9–16.3, roughly flat off the 15.67 prior close; still contained despite the chip rout
Big events (next 1–3 days) — UNH beat, Netflix AMC tonight, retail sales & claims today; big banks & more mega-caps next week
World risk — live U.S.–Iran conflict, fresh strikes, naval blockade reinstated, interim peace deal unraveled
Volatility trend — front-month VIX near 3-month; curve roughly normal but sensitive to any chip/Iran shock
Commodities — oil near 1-month highs ~$80, fourth up day on Hormuz risk; the biggest swing factor

snapshot.delta: Dow +0.29%, Nasdaq +0.62% Wed; chips lead down today
Nasdaq 100 ~−0.7% · chip rout, Dow buoyed by UNH
eased on soft CPI/PPI · oil caps the drop

setups.read: The day’s cleanest Sniper: a genuine double-beat — adjusted EPS $6.38 vs ~$4.85 est, revenue $112B — with full-year guidance raised to $19.50–$20.00, gapping UNH up ~7% premarket. Earnings risk is now behind it (Type A), and history says UNH beats tend to get paid. Cautions: medical costs remain elevated by management’s own words, and this is a big single-day gap into a jumpy tape — confirm the gap holds and place the short put well below the deal-day range. Grade B on those caveats.
setups.check_note: Pre-9:35. Confirm UNH holds above prior close & open, upper third of range at 9:35; half size only, short put well below the gap-day low.

setups.read: Money is rotating out of chips into defensive Dow names, and the UNH print reinforces the healthcare bid. But this is a broad rotation, not a single-name news gap — no clean per-stock catalyst outside UNH, and the driver is a risk-off shuffle that can reverse the moment chips stabilize. Watch-only; take the catalyst you can name (UNH), not the flow.
setups.check_note: A rotation isn’t a catalyst. Trade the named gap (UNH), not the sector drift.

setups.read: The fundamental lean is down — SK Hynix fell 11% in Seoul, TSMC’s raised capex spooked the AI trade, and SMH is off ~2.2% with Arm down ~4%. But two problems: a violent selloff like this snaps back hard on any stabilization headline, and the group has whipsawed all week. Selling bear calls into a fast-moving, gapping decline is exactly the squeeze risk 4-VAL exists to prevent.
setups.check_note: Fast, gapping selloff = snap-back risk. Don’t chase the short; wait for the tape to settle.

setups.read: Netflix reports after the close today, so earnings risk is ahead, not behind — that disqualifies it as a Type A right now and blocks any spread whose DTE spans tonight’s print. High single-stock gap risk into a jumpy macro tape. Check back once NFLX has reported and gapped.
setups.check_note: Earnings tonight = do not open any NFLX spread. Nothing to validate pre-print.

setups.read: A chip-rout, split-tape morning is a poor backdrop for clean breakouts. Apple made a new all-time high Tuesday, but the mega-cap tape is now under chip-driven pressure and no fresh high-volume ATH breakout is holding into today’s open. Bull-put breakouts need a calm, bid tape; today isn’t it.
setups.check_note: No qualifying breakout holding its pivot into a chip-selloff open — nothing to check.

scenarios.label: Most likely — Split, headline-driven chop
scenarios.sub: Nasdaq pressured by chips, Dow held up by UNH/defensives; range-bound, vol stays moderate

scenarios.label: Good case — Chip stabilization + earnings relief
scenarios.sub: The memory rout finds a floor, UNH-led rotation broadens, a relief drift higher, VIX eases

scenarios.label: Bad case — Chip rout deepens / Iran escalates
scenarios.sub: AI-spend fear spreads, or a fresh Iran/Hormuz strike; oil runs, equities gap down, VIX pops

scenarios.label: Tail — Hormuz closure / wider war
scenarios.sub: A strait shutdown or regional spread; oil spikes hard, a sharp VIX blow-off across risk assets

risks: Semiconductor rout — SK Hynix fell 11% in Seoul, the Kospi dropped over 6%, and TSMC’s raised capex spooked the AI trade. If the memory selloff spreads, it drags the whole index — the number-one thing to watch today.

Iran / Strait of Hormuz — the U.S. ran fresh strikes Wednesday and reinstated a naval blockade; the interim peace deal has unraveled. Any actual attack on shipping or bases sends crude and jet fuel vertical — the week’s biggest tail.
Oil near one-month highs & the 10-year — WTI ~$80 keeps inflation risk alive even after cooler CPI/PPI; the 10Y is ~4.57–4.59% and markets still price ~44–50% odds of a September Fed hike.

Netflix (tonight) & earnings cluster — NFLX reports after the close with high gap potential; UNH already gapped. Single-stock earnings gaps stacked on macro risk — keep spreads clear of each name’s print.
Retail sales & jobless claims · today — June retail sales and weekly claims land this morning; a surprise on either shapes the growth read and can add to the chop.

earnings.intro: Q2 season is ramping — UnitedHealth blew past estimates before the bell and Netflix reports after the close tonight, with the big banks and more mega-caps due next week. With chips selling off and the Iran backdrop still live, a single-stock earnings gap on top of macro risk is a double catalyst — keep every spread clear of its name’s print.

rows.why: Adjusted EPS $6.38 vs ~$4.85 est, revenue $112B, and full-year guidance raised to $19.50–$20.00 — a clean double-beat that sent the stock up ~7%. Earnings risk is now behind it. Management flagged still-elevated medical costs, the one caveat.
rows.angle: Earnings risk is behind UNH — a beat-and-hold is the day’s cleanest Type A. Confirm the gap holds at 9:35 before selling a bull put, half size.

rows.why: Reports after the close — earnings risk is ahead, not behind. High single-stock gap risk into a jumpy, chip-pressured tape. The number, not the setup, will drive it.
rows.angle: Do not carry any NFLX spread through tonight’s print. A clean gap could become a Type A tomorrow.

rows.why: The season broadens next week with more financials and the first mega-cap tech prints. Stacking single-stock risk on a still-live Iran backdrop and a jumpy chip tape — any bank/tech spread must expire before its print.
rows.angle: Keep any spread to a DTE that clears the earnings date — and mind the still-live geopolitical tail.

earnings.outro: Thu 7/16: UnitedHealth’s beat and the chip rout headline the day; Netflix reports after the close, with June retail sales and jobless claims this morning. It stays busy into next week with big banks and the first mega-caps — against a still-live Iran backdrop. Half size, A-grade only, until the tape settles.
